name: Firebase Authentication
abstract: Google-backed authentication service. Base auth is free with no MAU limit. Upgrading to Identity Platform adds MFA, multi-tenancy, SAML, and OIDC at $0.0015/MAU at scale. Cheapest option for high-volume applications.

featureList.description: Google, Apple, Facebook, Twitter/X, GitHub, Microsoft, Yahoo, Game Center, Play Games.
No native passkey/WebAuthn sign-in in Firebase Auth SDKs. Third-party extensions (e.g., Corbado) or a custom OIDC provider workaround are the only options.
Email OTP (one-time code) as a distinct passwordless sign-in method is not supported. Email link (magic link) is the only email-based passwordless flow.
Phone OTP via SMS. Billed per use on the Blaze (pay-as-you-go) plan.
Email link (magic link) sign-in for passwordless authentication.
Create anonymous user accounts that can be converted to permanent accounts on sign-up.
TOTP MFA available via Firebase Identity Platform (requires upgrade from base Firebase Auth).
Email verification is required for MFA, but it cannot be used as the second factor.
SMS MFA via Firebase Identity Platform.
Not supported natively. Enroll multiple factors to prevent lockout.
Not supported natively. Custom implementation via Firebase Cloud Messaging required.
Not supported natively. Implement via custom Blocking Functions.
No native integration.
Supported via reauthenticateWithCredential() and incremental MFA enrollment/verification patterns.
OIDC provider support via Firebase Identity Platform.
SAML provider support via Firebase Identity Platform.
Only SAML 2.0 and OIDC are supported as federated protocols; WS-Federation is not.
Custom tokens signed by a Google service account can authenticate backend services to Firebase. No native OAuth 2.0 client credentials (M2M) flow.
OIDC enterprise identity provider connections via Identity Platform.
SAML enterprise identity provider connections via Identity Platform.
No native LDAP/Active Directory connector. AD users can federate via SAML or OIDC (e.g., Azure AD as an IdP).
Microsoft/Azure AD via the built-in Microsoft OAuth provider or as a generic OIDC/SAML provider via Identity Platform.
Import password hashes in bcrypt, scrypt, standard/modified PBKDF2, SHA, HMAC, and MD5.
deleteUsers() accepts up to 1,000 UIDs per call; getUsers() accepts up to 100 identifiers. Available since Admin Node.js SDK v8.12.0.
importUsers() does not perform upserts. It processes users without deduplication; importing an existing UID/email fails for that record. Use updateUser() individually to update existing accounts.

Firebase Authentication does not have a native username field. Display names are stored as displayName (no uniqueness or format constraints), but true username-based auth requires custom Firestore logic. importUsers() supports uid (up to 128 chars), email, and phone but not a separate username field.
importUsers() does not support importing pre-existing TOTP or SMS MFA enrollments. Users must re-enroll MFA after migration.
No native SCIM endpoint. User lifecycle management must use the Admin SDK REST API or third-party bridges.
No outbound SCIM provisioning to downstream systems.
No group/role objects via SCIM. Groups must be implemented via custom claims or Firestore.
Users are created automatically on first sign-in via social/SAML/OIDC providers. Blocking functions allow custom logic during this flow.
Gradual migration via blocking functions that call an existing auth system.
FirebaseUI provides a basic profile update flow. Custom profile management requires building your own UI.
Link multiple authentication providers to a single Firebase user account.
Disable user accounts to prevent sign-in.
Store custom data in Firebase Realtime Database, Firestore, or user custom claims.
Custom claims for app-managed metadata (server-controlled).
Custom claims limited to 1000 bytes per user.
List users with pagination. Limited search capabilities via Admin SDK.
Role-based access implemented via custom claims. No built-in RBAC UI or API.
Multi-tenancy via separate Identity Platform tenants. Basic B2B model, no native organization abstraction.
Password policy configuration in Firebase Console.

Firebase Auth has no native username field with format restrictions. The displayName field is free-form. Custom username enforcement requires application-level logic (e.g., Firestore uniqueness checks).
No built-in progressive profiling flow. Can be implemented with custom UI and Blocking Functions.
No native ABAC. Implement via Firebase Security Rules and custom claims.
Custom claims and Firebase Security Rules for API/resource authorization.
Google Cloud HIPAA BAA covers Firebase Identity Platform.
Firebase Authentication is not FedRAMP authorized. Use GCP Identity Platform via Government Cloud.
Delete auth records via Admin SDK deleteUser(). Application data in Firestore/Storage/RTDB must be deleted separately.
No built-in consent management or cookie consent flows. Must be implemented at the application layer.
Firebase projects run in a selected Google Cloud region. Firestore data can be replicated multi-region.
Firebase Authentication is a fully managed Google Cloud service; self-hosted or private-cloud deployment is not supported.
Firebase Auth logs via Google Cloud Audit Logs on Identity Platform.
Logs exportable to BigQuery and Cloud Logging.
No dedicated security dashboard or threat intelligence center. Security signals are available via Google Cloud Audit Logs and Identity Platform's reCAPTCHA integration.
Customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) available via Identity Platform on Cloud KMS.

Identity Platform integrates natively with reCAPTCHA Enterprise for bot detection and SMS toll-fraud protection. App Check with reCAPTCHA Enterprise/v3 can also protect all Firebase backend endpoints.
Firebase automatically throttles sign-in attempts and returns auth/too-many-requests when thresholds are exceeded. Email enumeration protection is also available.
No built-in suspicious-IP throttling dashboard or policy. Implement via Blocking Functions or App Check with reCAPTCHA Enterprise.
No breached/compromised password detection (e.g., Have I Been Pwned integration). Must be implemented at the application layer.
No credential stuffing or credential-guard feature. App Check and reCAPTCHA Enterprise can partially mitigate automated credential attacks.
No tenant-level access control lists. Per-tenant isolation is achieved via Identity Platform multi-tenancy, but fine-grained tenant ACLs are not supported natively.
No native device fingerprinting. reCAPTCHA Enterprise signals (via Identity Platform) provide some device-level risk assessment.
No per-tenant/org custom branding in the hosted UI. Each Identity Platform tenant can have its own sign-in providers but shares the same FirebaseUI theme unless a fully custom UI is built per tenant.
MFA policies cannot be configured per tenant/organization in Identity Platform multi-tenancy.
FirebaseUI provides a hosted authentication flow.
Firebase Auth SDK for embedded login. FirebaseUI for ready-made components.
Full custom branding with FirebaseUI or custom UI.
FirebaseUI supports multiple languages.
FirebaseUI open-source components for web, iOS, and Android.
Auth events tracked via Firebase Analytics and BigQuery export.
Firebase SDKs for JavaScript, iOS (Swift/ObjC), Android (Kotlin/Java), Flutter, C++, Unity, and admin SDKs for Node.js, Python, Go, Java, .NET.
Firebase Admin SDK and REST API for user management.

Documented per-operation rate limits (e.g., account creation, sign-in). Limits can be tightened from the Google Cloud console. The auth/too-many-requests error is returned when thresholds are exceeded.
Blocking functions (beforeCreate, beforeSignIn) for custom auth logic. Cloud Functions for post-event hooks.
Cloud Functions for Firebase supports TypeScript.
Custom domain via Firebase Hosting.
Firebase CLI for deploying auth rules and functions.
Google Cloud Terraform provider covers some Firebase Identity Platform resources.
Firebase Auth uses its own user store. Custom database connections not supported.
Blocking functions and Cloud Functions Firestore triggers act as event hooks.
FirebaseUI is fully customizable with custom styles and strings.
Firebase uses Google's email delivery. Custom SMTP not supported in base Auth; available via Action Mailer extensions.
Customize email templates (sender name, address, subject, body) for password reset, email verification, and email change in the Firebase Console. Custom action URLs are also configurable.
Custom claims added via Admin SDK. Limited to 1000 bytes total.
No visual/no-code authentication flow builder. FirebaseUI provides pre-built components but requires code-level integration.
No built-in identity proofing or document verification. Must be integrated at the application layer via third-party services.
No support for DIDs, verifiable credentials, or W3C decentralized identity standards.
No native billing or subscription management integration. Billing is handled via Google Cloud billing accounts.
No native OAuth 2.0 M2M/agent identity flows or MCP-spec authorization. The Firebase MCP server for developer tooling uses CLI credentials, not a standards-based agentic auth model.
